originalTitle: Man dies after falling from Eleventh Night bonfire in east Belfast
summary: Warren Lyttle, who was in his 40s, died from his injuries after incident on the Braniel estate on Friday
content: A man in his 40s has died after falling from an Eleventh Night bonfire in Belfast, police said. The incident occurred on the Braniel estate in the east of the city on Friday night. In a statement on social media, the man was named as Warren Lyttle. He was taken to hospital on Friday and died from his injuries on Saturday. A statement from the Police Service of Northern Ireland said: “Police received a report at 4.40pm today, Saturday 11 July, that a man, aged in his 40s, had died in hospital following a fall at the site of a bonfire in east Belfast. “Police are investigating the circumstances of what happened, and the Health and Safety Executive for Northern Ireland has been informed.” A statement from the Braniel Bonfire Committee, Braniel Loyal Flute Band and Braniel Community Group said: “Our thoughts, prayers and deepest sympathies are with Warren’s family, friends and everyone who knew and loved him at this incredibly difficult time. “As a community, we are heartbroken by this tragic loss and ask that everyone keeps his loved ones in their prayers in the days and weeks ahead.” The statement said that, after discussions with Lyttle’s family, the fire would be lit as planned on Saturday night. It said: “We respectfully ask everyone attending to join us in observing a minute’s silence in Warren’s memory before the bonfire is lit.” A number of the traditional Eleventh Night bonfires were lit across Northern Ireland late on Friday with further pyres being ignited on Saturday and Sunday evenings. The bonfires are ignited before the Orange Order’s 12 July parades across Northern Ireland on Monday, which mark the Battle of the Boyne in 1690, when the Protestant King William of Orange defeated the Catholic King James. While most of the bonfires pass without incident, several have become the focus of contention due to the placing of flags, effigies and election posters on the structures before they are ignited. There was widespread condemnation after a bonfire in Moygashel, County Tyrone, which featured a replica of a mosque, was lit on Thursday before police could remove the display. A man appeared in court on Friday charged with incitement to hatred.
chineseTitle: 贝尔法斯特东部，一名男子从第十一夜篝火中坠落身亡
chineseSummary: 40 多岁的沃伦·利特尔 (Warren Lyttle) 在周五布兰尼尔庄园发生的事件中受伤身亡。
chineseContent: 警方称，一名 40 多岁的男子从贝尔法斯特的第十一夜篝火中坠落身亡。这起事件于周五晚上发生在该市东部的布兰尼尔庄园。在社交媒体上的一份声明中，该男子被命名为沃伦·利特尔(Warren Lyttle)。他于周五被送往医院，并于周六因伤重不治身亡。北爱尔兰警察局发表声明称：“7月11日星期六，今天下午4点40分，警方接到报告称，一名40多岁的男子在贝尔法斯特东部的篝火现场摔倒，在医院死亡。警方正在调查事件的具体情况，并已通知北爱尔兰健康与安全执行局。”布兰尼尔篝火委员会、布兰尼尔忠诚长笛乐队和布兰尼尔社区团体发表声明说：“在这个极其困难的时刻，我们向沃伦的家人、朋友以及所有认识和爱他的人表示哀悼、祈祷和最深切的同情。” “作为一个社区，我们对这一悲惨的损失感到心碎，并要求每个人在未来的几天和几周内为他所爱的人祈祷。”声明称，在与利特尔的家人讨论后，火将按计划于周六晚上点燃。上面写道：“我们恭请所有出席的人在篝火点燃前与我们一起默哀一分钟，以纪念沃伦。”周五晚间，北爱尔兰各地点燃了许多传统的第十一夜篝火，周六和周日晚上又点燃了更多的柴堆。周一，奥兰治骑士团在北爱尔兰举行 7 月 12 日游行之前，篝火被点燃，这场游行标志着 1690 年的博因河战役，当时信奉新教的奥兰治国王威廉击败了天主教国王詹姆斯。虽然大多数篝火都平安无事地过去了，但也有一些
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
